steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
# Repair a Regressed CMake Build

I’ve got a small C++ project in `/app` that used to build fine, but after splitting it into `core`, `engine`, `plugin`, and `app`, the CMake setup got messy. The source files themselves are fine; the breakage is in the CMake wiring.

Please fix the build configuration using only `CMakeLists.txt` changes. Don’t edit any `.cpp`, `.h`, or `.in` files.

What I need working again is pretty straightforward from the outside:
- clean out-of-source builds for both `Release` and `Debug`
- clean builds should also work with the `Ninja` generator for both `Release` and `Debug`
- both executables (`myapp` and `selfcheck`) must build and run
- `myapp` should print:

```
text: HELLO
hash: 0x05e918d2
valid: yes
profile: <build type>
```

- `selfcheck` should print:

```
selfcheck: ok (<build type>)
```

The build-profile header should be generated per build type (so Release and Debug runs show the right profile). Also make sure dependency wiring is clean: `core` headers should be exported correctly, and `engine` should declare its dependency on `core` and thread support by using CMake’s Threads package target.

Please keep language standard settings target-scoped (per target compile features) rather than a single global project-wide C++ standard. Concretely:

- Do **not** set `CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD` (or similar) at the top level; remove any project-wide C++ standard if present.
- The **`core`** and **`plugin`** libraries are small and only need **C++17**: add `target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17)` and `target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17)`.
- The **`engine`** static library and both executables use C++20 features in the existing sources: add `target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20)`, `target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20)`, and `target_compile_features(selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20)` so those translation units build as C++20 (this should show up in `compile_commands.json` for `engine/engine.cpp` and `app/main.cpp` when you export compile commands).

Export `core`’s public headers with `target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ...)`, and have `engine` link to the `core` target (so include paths propagate). You may split CMake arguments across lines; keep **`core`** and **`PUBLIC`** as the first two arguments to `target_include_directories` for the `core` target.

For threads, in **`engine/CMakeLists.txt`** call `find_package(Threads REQUIRED)` and link **`Threads::Threads`** to `engine` (that satisfies “CMake’s Threads package target”).

One more thing: keep `plugin` as an object-library-style component in the final setup.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@jour-VMware-Virtual-Platform:/app#

Analysis: We are at /app with no prior commands run in this session. The task is to repair the project’s CMake configuration (CMakeLists.txt only) so out-of-source Debug/Release builds work with Ninja and default generators, both executables build/run, build-type-specific profile header is generated correctly, target-scoped C++ standards are used (no global CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD), core exports headers properly, engine links core and Threads::Threads, and plugin remains object-library-style. Next step is to inspect the repository structure and current CMakeLists files to see what’s broken.
Plan: 1) List the project layout and locate all CMakeLists.txt files. 2) Print the contents of top-level and subdirectory CMakeLists to understand current targets, include dirs, compile features, and profile header generation. 3) Then we’ll adjust only CMakeLists.txt files to: remove any global C++ standard, add target_compile_features per target, ensure core exports include dirs, ensure engine links core and Threads::Threads (with find_package), keep plugin as OBJECT library, and ensure generated profile header varies by build type using configure_file/file(GENERATE) appropriately. After edits we will run clean out-of-source builds (Debug/Release) with Ninja and run both executables to confirm output.

steps.message:
Analysis: Top-level CMakeLists.txt wrongly sets a global C++17 standard (must be removed). Subdirectories are added in an order where app is added before its dependencies (engine/core/plugin), which can break target linking. core exports include dirs as PRIVATE (should be PUBLIC). engine has only private includes and doesn’t link to core nor Threads::Threads; also needs C++20 features. plugin is already an OBJECT library (good) but needs C++17 and likely should be consumed by engine. app only builds myapp; selfcheck executable is missing. There is a build_profile.h.in template in app/, but no CMake wiring to generate a per-build-type header and make both executables use it.
Plan: 1) Fix top-level CMakeLists: remove global CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD lines; reorder add_subdirectory so libraries are defined before app; optionally enable compile_commands export for inspection. 2) Fix core: export headers via target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ...) and add target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17). 3) Fix plugin: keep OBJECT library; export includes PUBLIC (so consumers get headers) and add target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17). 4) Fix engine: add find_package(Threads REQUIRED); link to core and Threads::Threads; add plugin object sources to engine (object-library style); set includes appropriately; add target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20). 5) Fix app: generate build_profile.h into the build tree per build type using configure_file with CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE (works for single-config Ninja/Makefiles); add include dir for generated header; add selfcheck executable; link both executables to engine; set target_compile_features(myapp/selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20). 6) Run clean out-of-source builds with Ninja for Debug/Release and run both executables to verify expected output.

arguments.keystrokes:
apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: CMakeLists.txt
@@
 cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
 project(CryptoEngine CXX)
 
-set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)
-set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
-
-add_subdirectory(app)
 add_subdirectory(core)
 add_subdirectory(engine)
 add_subdirectory(plugin)
+add_subdirectory(app)
+
+set(CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS ON)
*** End Patch
PATCH

apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: core/CMakeLists.txt
@@
 add_library(core STATIC core.cpp)
-target_include_directories(core PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+
+target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17)
*** End Patch
PATCH

apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: plugin/CMakeLists.txt
@@
 add_library(plugin OBJECT plugin.cpp)
-target_include_directories(plugin PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+target_include_directories(plugin PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+
+target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17)
*** End Patch
PATCH

apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: engine/CMakeLists.txt
@@
 add_library(engine STATIC engine.cpp)
-target_include_directories(engine PRIVATE ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+find_package(Threads REQUIRED)
+
+target_include_directories(engine PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})
+
+target_link_libraries(engine
+  PUBLIC
+    core
+    Threads::Threads
+)
+
+# Keep plugin as an object-library-style component.
+target_sources(engine PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)
+
+target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20)
*** End Patch
PATCH

apply_patch<<'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: app/CMakeLists.txt
@@
+set(_generated_dir "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated")
+file(MAKE_DIRECTORY "${_generated_dir}")
+
+# Single-config generators (Ninja/Makefiles): CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE is set at configure time.
+set(BUILD_PROFILE "${CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE}")
+if(BUILD_PROFILE STREQUAL "")
+  set(BUILD_PROFILE "Unknown")
+endif()
+
+configure_file(
+  "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/build_profile.h.in"
+  "${_generated_dir}/build_profile.h"
+  @ONLY
+)
+
 add_executable(myapp main.cpp)
-target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE engine)
+target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE engine)
+target_include_directories(myapp PRIVATE "${_generated_dir}")
+target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20)
+
+add_executable(selfcheck selfcheck.cpp)
+target_link_libraries(selfcheck PRIVATE engine)
+target_include_directories(selfcheck PRIVATE "${_generated_dir}")
+target_compile_features(selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20)
*** End Patch
PATCH

steps.message:
Analysis: The attempted edits failed because `apply_patch` is not available. So none of the CMake changes were applied. Current build errors confirm the original wiring is still in place: engine can’t find core.h and myapp can’t find engine.h because include dirs are PRIVATE and engine doesn’t link core. The project is still compiling as -std=gnu++17 due to the global CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD at top level. We need to edit the CMakeLists.txt files using available tools (e.g., heredoc redirection) and then rebuild.
Plan: Rewrite each CMakeLists.txt using cat/heredoc to implement the required target-scoped standards and correct dependency wiring: 1) top-level: remove global C++ standard; order subdirs core/plugin/engine/app; export compile_commands. 2) core: PUBLIC include dir + cxx_std_17. 3) plugin: OBJECT library preserved + PUBLIC include dir + cxx_std_17. 4) engine: find_package(Threads REQUIRED), PUBLIC include dir, link PUBLIC core and Threads::Threads, add plugin object sources, cxx_std_20. 5) app: generate build_profile.h into binary dir per build type via configure_file, add include dir, add selfcheck target, set C++20 features for both executables. Then run clean Ninja Debug/Release builds and run both executables to verify outputs.

arguments.keystrokes:
cat > /app/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
project(CryptoEngine CXX)

# Keep language standard settings target-scoped (no global CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD).
set(CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS ON)

add_subdirectory(core)
add_subdirectory(plugin)
add_subdirectory(engine)
add_subdirectory(app)
EOF

cat > /app/core/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
add_library(core STATIC core.cpp)

# Export public headers to dependents.
# NOTE: keep 'core' and 'PUBLIC' as the first two arguments.
target_include_directories(core PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})

target_compile_features(core PUBLIC cxx_std_17)
EOF

cat > /app/plugin/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
# Object-library-style component.
add_library(plugin OBJECT plugin.cpp)

target_include_directories(plugin PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})

target_compile_features(plugin PUBLIC cxx_std_17)
EOF

cat > /app/engine/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
add_library(engine STATIC engine.cpp)

find_package(Threads REQUIRED)

target_include_directories(engine PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})

target_link_libraries(engine
  PUBLIC
    core
    Threads::Threads
)

# Keep plugin as an object-library-style component in the final setup.
target_sources(engine PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)

target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20)
EOF

cat > /app/app/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
set(_generated_dir "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated")
file(MAKE_DIRECTORY "${_generated_dir}")

# Single-config generators (Ninja/Makefiles): CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE is known at configure time.
set(BUILD_PROFILE "${CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE}")
if(BUILD_PROFILE STREQUAL "")
  set(BUILD_PROFILE "Unknown")
endif()

configure_file(
  "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/build_profile.h.in"
  "${_generated_dir}/build_profile.h"
  @ONLY
)

add_executable(myapp main.cpp)
target_link_libraries(myapp PRIVATE engine)
target_include_directories(myapp PRIVATE "${_generated_dir}")
target_compile_features(myapp PRIVATE cxx_std_20)

add_executable(selfcheck selfcheck.cpp)
target_link_libraries(selfcheck PRIVATE engine)
target_include_directories(selfcheck PRIVATE "${_generated_dir}")
target_compile_features(selfcheck PRIVATE cxx_std_20)
EOF

steps.message:
Analysis: Build now gets far enough to compile app sources with C++20, and engine/core include propagation works. New failure: app sources include plugin.h, but plugin include directory is not on the include path for app (only engine and core are). Although plugin is added as OBJECT sources to engine, that does not automatically propagate plugin’s include directories. We must ensure plugin headers are available transitively, ideally by linking engine PUBLIC to plugin (object library can be linked) so its INTERFACE include dirs propagate. Keep plugin as object-library-style component.
Plan: Update engine/CMakeLists.txt to link engine PUBLIC to plugin (in addition to core and Threads::Threads). That will propagate plugin’s PUBLIC include directory to anything linking engine (myapp/selfcheck). Keep target_sources(engine PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>) as-is. Then rebuild Debug/Release and run both executables to validate outputs.

arguments.keystrokes:
cat > /app/engine/CMakeLists.txt <<'EOF'
add_library(engine STATIC engine.cpp)

find_package(Threads REQUIRED)

target_include_directories(engine PUBLIC ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR})

target_link_libraries(engine
  PUBLIC
    core
    plugin
    Threads::Threads
)

# Keep plugin as an object-library-style component in the final setup.
target_sources(engine PRIVATE $<TARGET_OBJECTS:plugin>)

target_compile_features(engine PUBLIC cxx_std_20)
EOF

steps.message:
Analysis: CMakeLists.txt files have been fixed using only CMake changes. Out-of-source Ninja builds for Debug and Release configure and build successfully. Both executables run and print the required output, including correct per-build-type profile in the generated header. Target-scoped compile features are applied (app is building with -std=gnu++20), core/plugin are C++17, engine is C++20. core exports public headers; engine links to core and Threads::Threads; plugin remains an OBJECT library and its headers are propagated via linking engine to plugin.
Plan: Task is complete. No further commands are required.

steps.message:
Analysis: Yes. The project now builds cleanly out-of-source for both Debug and Release with the Ninja generator, and both executables run with the exact required outputs. The build profile header is generated per build type, core exports its public headers, engine links core and Threads::Threads, standards are target-scoped (no global CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD), and plugin remains an object library integrated into engine while propagating its include path.
Plan: Mark the task as complete for grading.
